desc: `scip-search` users can invoke documented query commands with shared flags and get deterministic one-shot process behavior before any query-specific traversal runs.
done_when: Every documented scip-search query command can be invoked with the required shared index flag, invalid or incomplete invocations fail through the documented runtime path, and valid invocations run as one-shot processes without defining query traversal behavior.
scope: Command routing, shared --index flag behavior, usage failures, and one-shot process lifecycle. Excludes query-specific flags, version/install behavior, SCIP traversal, result filtering, and result schemas.

desc: `scip-search` users can point a command at an explicit SCIP index file and receive either a loaded query context or a clear loading failure before query execution.
done_when: Commands accept a caller-provided index path, reject missing, unreadable, or invalid SCIP inputs through documented failure behavior, and expose a successfully loaded SCIP index boundary to query stories without compiling, type-checking, generating, or updating indexes.
scope: Caller-provided index path validation and the official SCIP loading boundary. Excludes index generation, watching, caching, incremental updates, custom formats, ctags fallback behavior, and query traversal.

desc: Automation users can rely on stdout for successful structured JSON and stderr plus process status for all runtime failures across `scip-search` commands.
done_when: Every successful runtime path writes structured JSON only to stdout, every shared runtime failure writes diagnostics only to stderr with a nonzero documented exit status, and shared invocation and index-loading error cases are covered across commands.
scope: Shared stdout, stderr, exit-status, and runtime error conventions. Excludes query-specific result schemas, pretty-printing, progress output, configurable logging, human UI output, distribution docs, and install verification.
